notes: Mistral Vibe's autonomous loop is the /loop scheduled self-prompt (vibe/core/loop.py): /loop 30s <prompt> re-fires the prompt on a >=30s timer, each fire a discrete turn submitted from idle when the input bar is focused, until /loop cancel all. This is autonomous multi-turn self-prompting (no user input between turns) but NOT goal-convergence — hence agent=partial and, like the claudecode sibling, the state arc is per-iteration ready→working→ready rather than one continuous working span. This spec captures the arc vibe actually produces: two scheduled fires, each a clean turn on the SAME session id, then /loop cancel returns the session to ready. Single-birth adapter (session id = the session_<ts>_<hash> dir via sessionIDFromPath), so session_birth is anchored to start UNPINNED and every later phase anchors forward. Observations: model mistral-medium-3.5 (live config.active_model default, NormalizeModelName unchanged) + tokens_nonzero (sidecar stats.context_tokens on turn_done); cost NOT asserted (adapter emits no per-turn cost). PROVISIONAL — parser+source read, no recording; record settles fire timing, the >=30s idle interval, and whether a proc-<PID> presession precedes the birth.

text: Session appears in ready — vibe writes messages.jsonl lazily, so the proc-<PID> presession or the session_<...> dir (whichever the scanner mints first) satisfies the birth
Spec: 'ready → working at loop start'. The first /loop fire (~30s after scheduling) injects the scheduled prompt with no user input — ready → working for iteration 1

Iteration 1 completes back to ready. Unlike claudecode's <500ms flicker, vibe holds ready for the >=30s scheduling interval before the next fire — the scenario's 'stays continuously working' bullet is relaxed the same way it was for claudecode
The second scheduled fire drives iteration 2 ready → working with no user input between turns — the autonomous loop is active (>= 2 turns)

Spec: 'after the goal is reached: working → ready'. /loop cancel all stops the scheduler; the session settles in ready. Vibe has no goal-convergence, so termination is the explicit cancel rather than a condition-met event. The only strict invariant is that the session never enters waiting (no permission gates).
